content.full_text:
State of California 
Memor ndum 
To 
from 
Subject: 
Opinion Request Meeting 
Date 
December 31, 1979 
FAIR PO~ITICAL PRACTICES COMM.' I@, ~14' Of 
John Glansello 
cl'f!Y~ 
I have already advised Mr. Hill that on the basis of the 
Ferraro opinion, 4 FPPC 62 (No. 78-009) Mr. Siemens 
would not be disqualified under the facts given. 
Mr. Hill 
wishes to ask whether there is any possibility of recon-
sideration and revision of the advice given in the Ferraro 
opinion. 


December 17, 1979 
Mr. John Giansello 
Fair Political Practices Commission 
P. O. Box 807 
Sacramento, California 95804 
Dear Mr. Giansello: 
The Town of Los Gatos is a general law city of the 
State of California. 
It has a five member city council 
(Town Council) elected at large. 
Peter Siemens is a 
councilmember. 
Mr. Siemens owns the house where he lives and, 
on the same lot, a small dwelling of the sort called a guest 
cottage. 
He rents the cottage for $220 monthly. 
Full market 
rent considering all factors would be more, but it is difficult 
to say how much. 
Mr,. Siemen's ownership interest in the 
cottage is certainly worth more than $1,000. 
The Town Council has adopted a three-month rent freeze 
ordinance affecting all rented dwellings except those on 
separate lots (including separately-saleable condominium units 
and townhouse units), and is considering whether to adopt a 
permanent rent control ordinance. 
Neither of two basic questions, 
whether to have a rent control ordinance at all, and what classes 
of dwellings, if any, ought to be exempted from a rent control 
ordinance, has been answered. 
It is reasonably foreseeable, 
although not certain, that any rent control ordinance would have 
a substantial effect on all rents for dwellings, both those which 
are controlled and those which might be exempted. 
Mr. Siemens has directed me to ask the FPPC whether he may 
participate in and attempt to influence the committee work and 
drafting of a rent control ordinance, and whether he may vote 
when the issues corne before the council. 
Very. truly yours, 
f 
PWH/j 
Town 
cc: 
Peter Siemens

sections.question: Mr. Siemens has directed me to ask the FPPC whether he may participate in and attempt to influence the committee work and drafting of a rent control ordinance, and whether he may vote when the issues come before the council.
sections.question_synthetic: Whether a city councilmember who owns a rental cottage can participate in and vote on the development and adoption of a rent control ordinance that would foreseeably affect the value of his property.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: Based on the Ferraro opinion (4 FPPC 62, No. 78-009), Mr. Siemens would not be disqualified from voting on the rent control ordinance under the circumstances presented.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Document contains both incoming request letter (dated Dec 17) and internal FPPC memorandum (dated Dec 31). The memorandum references prior FPPC advice already given based on Ferraro opinion rather than providing new detailed conclusion. Full reasoning and analysis section not present in provided text. Question section taken verbatim from incoming letter. Conclusion represents the advice already provided, not a full new opinion.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: Mr. Siemens has directed me to ask the FPPC whether he may participate in and attempt to influence the committee work and drafting of a rent control ordinance, and whether he may vote when the issues come before the council.

CONCLUSION: Based on the Ferraro opinion (4 FPPC 62, No. 78-009), Mr. Siemens would not be disqualified from voting on the rent control ordinance under the circumstances presented.

December 17, 1979 Mr. John Giansello Fair Political Practices Commission P. O. Box 807 Sacramento, California 95804 Dear Mr. Giansello: The Town of Los Gatos is a general law city of the State of California. It has a five member city council (Town Council) elected at large. Peter Siemens is a councilmember. Mr. Siemens owns the house where he lives and, on the same lot, a small dwelling of the sort called a guest cottage. He rents the cottage for $220 monthly. Full market rent considering all factors would be more, but it is difficult to say how much. Mr,. Siemen's ownership interest in the cottage is certainly worth more than $1,000. The Town Council has adopted a three-month rent freeze ordinance affecting all rented dwellings except those on separate lots (including separately-saleable condominium units and townhouse units), and is considering whether to adopt a permanent rent control ordinance. Neither of two basic questions, whether to have a rent control ordinance at all, and what classes of dwellings, if any, ought to be exempted from a rent control ordinance, has been answered. embedding.summary: A memorandum regarding whether City Councilmember Peter Siemens of Los Gatos can participate in deliberations and voting on a proposed rent control ordinance given his ownership interest in a rental cottage that would be affected by such regulation.
